description: Fix the /eval runner (.claude/skills/eval/run.sh) to build evals/RESULTS.md into a temp file and replace it with a single atomic mv -f, reading carry-forward rows from a pre-write snapshot — eliminating partial-write corruption and filtered-run carry-forward erasure.

userStories.title: Atomic temp-file write + carry-forward fix in run.sh
userStories.description: As the autopilot eval gate, I want RESULTS.md rewritten atomically from a temp file with carry-forward rows read from a pre-write snapshot, so a crash or concurrent run never leaves a partial scoreboard and a filtered run never erases untouched rows.
acceptanceCriteria: Before the write block, run.sh captures the original RESULTS.md content once with a set -e-safe idiom that cannot abort on a missing file: RESULTS_ORIG=""; [ -f "$RESULTS" ] && RESULTS_ORIG="$(cat "$RESULTS")" (or equivalent).

prior_row()/prior_status() resolve carry-forward from the RESULTS_ORIG snapshot (e.g. grep ... <<<"$RESULTS_ORIG"), NOT from the live $RESULTS; every grep over the snapshot keeps a trailing || true so a no-match cannot abort under set -euo pipefail; prior_row()'s body contains no live grep/[ -f ] read of $RESULTS after capture.

tmp is initialized empty (tmp="") BEFORE the --ablate guard and the EXIT trap is the guarded form trap '[ -n "$tmp" ] && rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT so it is harmless on the ablation exec path (run.sh:49); the real temp path tmp="$RESULTS.tmp.$$" is a SIBLING of $RESULTS in evals/ (never a /tmp/ path) and is assigned at the start of the write block.

The header heredoc and every per-probe row append write to $tmp not $RESULTS; the file is replaced in one step with mv -f "$tmp" "$RESULTS" after all rows and the trailing benchmark comment; no bare cat > "$RESULTS" truncation remains anywhere in the script.

Structural equivalence: a full run (no filter) produces a RESULTS.md identical to a pre-change full run except for the per-run last-run (UTC) timestamp column — same header, same row set and order (probe glob order), same trailing benchmark comment, same regressions-to-stdout summary, same exit code (0 normally, 1 on green->red regression per PR #32). Verify by diffing with the timestamp column masked.

The if [ "${#regressions[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then exit 1; fi block stays within the LAST 12 LINES of run.sh (eval-runner-exit.sh asserts this via tail -n 12); if the refactor would push it out, eval-runner-exit.sh is updated in this same story so it does not falsely REGRESSION.
A filtered run (bash .claude/skills/eval/run.sh --probe <id>) leaves every other probe's row intact (carried from RESULTS_ORIG), not reset to (not run).
bash -n .claude/skills/eval/run.sh passes and the existing scripts/__tests__ vitest suite stays green (run: pnpm test:scripts).
userStories.notes: iter 1 (2026-06-13): run.sh now builds RESULTS.md into $RESULTS.tmp.$$ and replaces via atomic mv -f; prior_row reads RESULTS_ORIG snapshot; guarded EXIT trap + tmp='' before --ablate guard; verified structural equivalence (masked diff identical), filtered-run carry-forward (0 '(not run)' vs OLD's 4), --ablate path intact, exit-1 guard in last 12 lines, eval-runner-exit PASS, pnpm test:scripts 186 green. commit 4fed39e.

userStories.title: Tier-A probe locking in the atomic-write invariant
userStories.description: As the harness fitness suite, I want a probe that fails if the non-atomic write pattern is reintroduced, so the US-001 fix cannot silently regress.
acceptanceCriteria: New executable file evals/probes/eval-results-atomic.sh with headers # tier: A, # source: issue #83 (eval-results-atomic-write), and a # desc: noting it is a STATIC-pattern probe coupled to run.sh implementation tokens (a deliberate refactor must update it) — matching evals/probes/eval-runner-exit.sh header style.

The probe statically asserts against .claude/skills/eval/run.sh: (a) no bare cat > "$RESULTS" truncation present, (b) mv -f ... "$RESULTS" present for the final replacement, (c) a trap ... EXIT referencing the temp file present, (d) the prior_row() function body contains no live grep ... "$RESULTS" read.
Probe exit-code oracle honored: exit 0 = PASS (all four invariants hold), exit 1 = REGRESSION (failing invariant named on stderr), exit 2 = SKIPPED (run.sh absent).
Behavioral check: the probe exits 0 against the US-001-refactored run.sh and exits 1 when the cat > "$RESULTS" truncation token is re-introduced (demonstrable against a doctored copy).
On the first run that includes it, the new probe's own row appears as PASS (not (not run)) and all other probe rows are preserved.
bash -n evals/probes/eval-results-atomic.sh passes and the scripts/__tests__ vitest suite stays green.
userStories.notes: iter 2 (2026-06-13): added evals/probes/eval-results-atomic.sh (tier A, source issue #83); statically asserts no `cat > "$RESULTS"`, mv -f present, EXIT trap referencing $tmp, prior_row() free of live $RESULTS grep. All 4 invariants fire independently (exit 1, named on stderr); exit 0 vs refactored run.sh, exit 1 vs doctored cat-truncation copy, exit 2 when run.sh absent; first --tier A run scored its row PASS with all 13 prior rows preserved (0 '(not run)'); bash -n OK, pnpm test:scripts 186 green. commit 07a5480.

userStories.title: Doc + CHANGELOG updates for the atomic-write semantics
userStories.description: As a maintainer reading the eval docs, I want the write-semantics prose to describe the atomic temp-rename pattern, so the docs match the code.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/README.md and .claude/skills/eval/SKILL.md describe the RESULTS.md write as build-into-a-temp-file-then-replace-atomically-with-mv-f; no surviving prose says truncate-then-append (or equivalent) for that write.
The SKILL.md carry-forward description (the step covering carrying prior rows for probes not run this invocation) states carry-forward reads the pre-write snapshot (RESULTS_ORIG), not the live file.
No behavioral spec wording changes: the exit-code oracle, the pass-rate formula, and the overwrite-row-per-probe policy keep their current intent (manual review item).
CHANGELOG.md gains exactly one entry under ### Fixed in the ## [Unreleased] section, imperative mood, linking issue #83; no other CHANGELOG sections are modified.

After all edits, regenerate the scoreboard once (bash .claude/skills/eval/run.sh) so the new eval-results-atomic row is scored, and stage the updated evals/RESULTS.md; the runner exits 0 (a pre-existing next-dev-prod red with unchanged delta is acceptable per the documented eval-gate rule).
